item.title: Chaperone-mediated autophagy is required for regulatory T cell function.
item.description: Nature communications - Chaperone-mediated autophagy (CMA) is a selective form of protein degradation in lysosomes that declines with age. Besides protein quality control, CMA also regulates several cellular processes thr...

item.title: Apical Periodontitis May Exacerbate Ulcerative Colitis via Neutrophil Extracellular Trap-Mediated Systemic Inflammation and Intestinal Barrier Disruption in Mice.
item.description: International endodontic journal - This study investigated the impact of apical periodontitis (AP) on ulcerative colitis (UC) progression and elucidated the roles of intestinal barrier dysfunction and neutrophil extracellular traps ...

item.title: Food-Derived Biohybrid Probiotic Extracellular Vesicles for Synergistic Therapy of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Small (Weinheim an der Bergstrasse, Germany) - Inflammatory bowel disease is a chronic gastrointestinal disorder characterized by persistent intestinal inflammation, microbiota dysbiosis, and impaired mucosal barrier function. Conventional trea...

item.title: Dihydroartemisinin alleviates dextran sulfate sodium-induced colitis in mice: Associations with gut microbiota, arachidonic acid metabolism, and the IL-17 signaling pathway.
item.description: Pathology, research and practice - Dihydroartemisinin (DHA), a sesquiterpene lactone derived from artemisinin, exhibits well-documented anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. However, the precise mechanisms through which DHA ...

item.title: Enhanced gastrointestinal stability and therapeutic efficacy of biofilm self-coated Bacillus amyloliquefaciens C-1 probiotic in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: NPJ science of food - Given the rising global incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and limited treatment options, probiotic efficacy is hindered by poor gastrointestinal survival. This study developed a self-co...

item.title: Microbiome-Directed Therapy for Fatigue in Quiescent Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Randomized Placebo-Controlled Trial of Multi-Strain Probiotic Supplementation.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Fatigue is a challenging symptom for patients with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). Emerging evidence links alterations in the gut microbiome with fatigue in IBD, highlighting the potential of mi...

item.title: Real-world use and perceptions of advanced combined therapies in treating inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Intestinal research - Combining therapies targeting distinct pathways shows promise in breaking the therapeutic ceiling for inflammatory bowel disease. The study explored real-world experiences and gaps between current ...

item.title: Periodontitis and Inflammatory Bowel Diseases: Mechanistic Evidence.
item.description: Journal of periodontal research - Review - Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is a multifactorial disorder characterized by chronic intestinal inflammation. Focusing on a single aspect of its pathogenesis is insufficient to capture the comple...

item.title: Fecal microbiome of patients with ulcerative colitis reflects their phenotype and inflammatory level.
item.description: Scientific reports - Inflammatory bowel diseases affect ever-increasing numbers of individuals worldwide. Alterations of the intestinal microbiome were reported for Crohn's disease and at relapse in Ulcerative Colitis ...

item.title: A Review of Curcumin in Chronic Disease Management: Anti-Inflammatory Pathways, Antioxidant Activity, and Therapeutic Advances.
item.description: Journal of nutrition and metabolism - Review - The medicinal properties of Curcuma longa L. (turmeric) have long been recognized, especially in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat diverse ailments for thousands of years. The b...

item.title: Combined acylated catalpol diglycoside and Lactobacillus therapy restores the miR-146a-TRAF6-SIRT1/AMPK signaling axis in experimental colitis.
item.description: Toxicology and applied pharmacology - Inflammatory bowel disease is characterized by altered innate immunity, oxidative stress, and gut microbiota imbalance. This study explored the association between miR-146a expression and the TRAF6...

item.title: Inorganic nitrogen metabolic reprogramming of the gut microbiome drives fecal microbiota transplantation in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Nature communications - Although fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) shows promise for ulcerative colitis (UC), its clinical success appears to be contingent upon the degree of donor microbiota engraftment. Using an LD...

item.title: Asiaticoside alleviates inflammation and intestinal barrier dysfunction in Crohn's disease-like colitis in a manner associated with the RhoA/ROCK/MLC pathway.
item.description: Naunyn-Schmiedeberg's archives of pharmacology - Asiaticoside (AS) improves colitis by inhibiting inflammation and protecting the intestinal barrier, but its role in Crohn's disease (CD) is still unclear. In this study, the TNBS-induced mouse col...

item.title: Gut Commensals Regulate the Intestinal Kynurenine Pathway.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - The kynurenine pathway (KP) of tryptophan metabolism shapes host immunity and is implicated in inflammatory, malignant, and neurodegenerative diseases. Although it is well known that inflammation i...

item.title: Structural characterization of an acetylated glucomannan from Aloe barbadensis and its alleviation of murine colitis via coordinated ECM repair and microbiota-metabolism actions.
item.description: Carbohydrate polymers - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease for which effective and durable therapeutic strategies remain limited. In this study, an acetylated glucomannan polysaccharide, Aloe ...

item.title: Gut microbiota-intestinal barrier crosstalk: mechanistic advances, disease relevance, and public health implications.
item.description: Frontiers in public health - Review - The intestinal barrier is a critical interface between the host and the external environment, and growing evidence indicates that bidirectional crosstalk between the gut microbiota and the intestin...
